title: Indian founder Zapier price complaint
author: indianfounder
body: Zapier is too expensive for Indian startups. $30/mo per seat, doesn't accept INR billing well, no UPI. We moved everything to n8n self-hosted on Hetzner, saving 90%.

title: D2C founder refund velocity pain
author: d2c_founder
body: Refunds on Razorpay take 5-7 days, customers furious. Need an agent to autoreply 'in transit'. Wish we had instant refund webhook automation that doesn't require building from scratch.

title: Tier-2 developer dream
author: tier2dev
body: Building from a tier-2 city in India. Spent 3 weekends wiring Cashfree webhooks to Slack to WhatsApp. An MCP that just does it would have saved me 30 hours.

title: SME owner reconciliation pain
author: sme_owner
body: Daily UPI reconciliation across 5 PGs is killing my finance team. Need an agent that pulls from Razorpay+Cashfree+PayU+ICICI+HDFC and matches against Tally. Doesn't exist as one solution.

title: Razorpay-OpenAI Codex partnership April 2026
author: Razorpay
body: Starting today, developers on Codex, OpenAI's AI coding platform used by over a million developers every week, can now connect Razorpay directly inside the agent and start accepting payments instantly. Build me an AI-powered fitness coaching app and help me collect payments from my users via Razorpay - one prompt, app generated, Razorpay payment gateway auto-set-up.

title: Shashank Kumar on Razorpay MCP launch
author: shashank_kr
body: The @Razorpay MCP Server is officially live! Razorpay becomes India's first payment gateway with an Official MCP (Model Context Protocol) Server, designed for an AI-first world. Connect to any AI system, whether it's a chat, an app, or a fully autonomous system

body: Indian AI startup Gnani.ai partnered with Razorpay to launch agentic AI collections platform that completes payment transactions during live customer calls. Combines Gnani's agentic AI systems with Razorpay's MCP server.

body: Razorpay MCP integration now available - expanding payment capabilities for builders in India. Connect through Replit projects to accept payments, manage orders, and handle refunds directly through Agent.

body: MCP Payments: How to Connect AI Agents to Payment Infrastructure. Stripe MCP, x402 crypto, AgentPay. The agentic payment stack is forming - India needs its own native variant.

author: PayU
body: PayU rolls out Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. Merchants can connect their AI assistants to PayU's payment systems. AI software and chatbots can securely access PayU platform APIs and complete actions.

title: Pine Labs OpenAI partnership
author: PineLabs
body: Pine Labs partners with OpenAI to build Agentic Commerce in India. Merchants and enterprises can leverage intelligent systems that actively manage transactions, supplier negotiations, and recurring payments. Opens agentic stack to developer ecosystem for third-party innovators.

title: Razorpay Agent Studio launch
author: Razorpay
body: Razorpay Agent Studio introduces AI agents that automatically detect problems and take action on your behalf. It connects deeply to your systems to solve problems even before they are raised. From dispute responders auto-file rebuttals with proofs. Subscription recovery agents.

title: Skeptical reaction to Razorpay AI
author: dev_skeptic
body: Razorpay has the worst webhooks docs in the industry. Adding AI on top of broken developer experience won't fix the underlying issue. Cashfree was easier to integrate.

title: fix(copilot): P0 guardrails, transient retry, and security hardening
author: majdyz
body:
### Why

The copilot's Claude Code CLI integration had several production reliability gaps reported from live deployments:

- **No transient retry**: 429 rate-limit errors, 5xx server errors, and ECONNRESET connection resets surfaced immediately as failures — there was no retry mechanism.
- **Subagent permission errors**: CLI subprocesses wrote temp files to `/tmp/claude-0/` which was inaccessible inside E2B sandboxes, causing subagent spawning to report "agent completed" without actually running.
- **Missing security hardening in non-OpenRouter modes**: Security env vars (`CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE

title: Why AI Agents Can't Be Trusted With Money — And What We Built to Fix It
author: qbitflow
body: Spot on. The UX of agentic payments usually breaks at the "authorization" layer. Traditional banks want a human with a 2FA token; agents need a cryptographically capped spending limit that they can deploy autonomously. Non-custodial smart contracts are the only way to let an agent be a consumer without giving it the keys to the kingdom.

title: fix(ai-builder): Stop tombstoning example UUIDs in LangSmith dataset sync (no-changelog)
author: JoseBra
body:
## Summary

Fixes the recurring `LangSmithConflictError: 409` that aborts eval runs during `syncDataset`. Two compounding design choices caused it:

1. **Deterministic UUIDs** derived from `${testCaseFile}/${scenarioName}`.
2. **Soft-delete on every full sync** for examples missing from the filesystem.

LangSmith soft-delete tombstones UUIDs permanently. Because our UUIDs were a pure function of the derivedId, any later sync reintroducing a previously-deleted scenario collided and the sync aborted before any evaluation could run. Branch rotation between teammates working on different scenario

title: [FEATURE] Add `when:` frontmatter field to SKILL.md for context-gated skill listing
author: trek-e
body:
## Preflight Checklist

- [x] I have searched [existing requests](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20label%3Aenhancement) and this feature hasn't been requested yet
- [x] This is a single feature request (not multiple features)

---

## Problem Statement

Every installed skill appears in the `system-reminder` skill listing on every session turn, regardless of whether it is relevant to the current project. A developer working in a Python repository pays the token cost of SwiftUI skills. A repository with no `.planning/` directory pays for 86 workflow-orchestration s

title: feat(backend/copilot): hide session-level dry_run from LLM
author: majdyz
body:
### Why

During autopilot sessions with \`dry_run=True\`, the LLM was leaking awareness of simulation mode through three channels:

1. \`dry_run\` appeared as a required parameter in \`RunBlockTool\`'s schema — the LLM could see and pass it.
2. \`is_dry_run: true\` appeared in the serialized MCP tool result JSON the LLM received, causing it to narrate that execution was simulated.
3. The \`[DRY RUN]\` prefix on response messages told the LLM explicitly that credentials were absent or execution was skipped.

This broke the illusion of a seamless preview experience: users watching an autopilot d

title: feat: Frontend platform linking page (/link/{token})
author: Bentlybro
body:
## Why
Completes the two linking flows from #12615 / #12618. When the bot sends a user a one-time `{frontend}/link/{token}?platform=...` URL, this page is where the user actually connects their AutoGPT account — whether that's claiming a server or linking their personal DMs.

## The flows

**Server link (`linkType: SERVER`)**
1. User runs `/setup` in a Discord guild → bot replies ephemerally with the link
2. Clicks link → logs in to AutoGPT if needed → lands here
3. Page shows: *"Set up AutoPilot for {ServerName}"* with a clear billing notice
4. Confirm → `POST /api/platform-linking/t

title: feat(data-warehouse): add webhook s3 sink to data_warehouse dev env
author: MarconLP
body:
## Problem

The local data warehouse dev env is missing the webhook S3 sink process needed when working on warehouse webhook sources (Slack, Stripe). Without it, webhook-driven schemas can't be exercised end-to-end locally.

## Changes

- Add the webhook S3 sink to `bin/mprocs.yaml` so it starts alongside the rest of the data warehouse dev processes.
- Update `devenv/intent-map.yaml` to include the sink under the `data_warehouse` intent.

Split out from #50928 so the dev env tweak can land independently of the Slack webhook source work.

## How did you test this code?

I'm an agent. No automat
